hTERT.promoter_state: Germline-edited with added HIF-1α response elements (HREs) and NFAT5 enhancers
hTERT.cold_adaptation: Psychrophilic surface mutations: Glu→Asp at 3 solvent-exposed residues for cold stability
hTERT.activation: Shelterin density drop + HIF-1α (hypoxia) + NFAT5 (osmotic stress)

telomerase.tissue_specificity: Enhanced in gill epithelium and hematopoietic cells; basal in other somatic tissues

ouroboric_telomere_endogenous.mechanism: Seven-layer endogenous loop with gill-specific enhancements
ouroboric_telomere_endogenous.intervention: GERMLINE — hTERT promoter edited with HREs + NFAT5 enhancers; cold-adapted hTERT mutations

layers.L1_SENSE: Shelterin density sensor — enhanced POT1 cold-stability for marine environments
layers.L2_SIGNAL: ATM → KAP1 → chromatin relaxation + HIF-1α + NFAT5 → multi-input activation
layers.L3_DEREPRESS: TET2-mediated CpG demethylation at hTERT promoter
layers.L4_EXPRESS: Conditional hTERT: basal (like endogenous) + HIF-1α boost during underwater hypoxia + NFAT5 boost during osmotic stress
layers.L5_EXTEND: Telomerase with cold-adapted processivity (maintains activity at 4–15°C)
layers.L6_TERMINATE: G-quadruplex length sensor; enhanced by salt (Na⁺ stabilizes parallel G4)
layers.L7_SEAL: CST + Pol α fill-in; T-loop reformation

hypoxia_driven_hTERT.mechanism: HIF-1α binds hypoxia response elements (HREs) engineered into hTERT promoter
hypoxia_driven_hTERT.trigger: Underwater periods (pO₂ < 40 mmHg in gill tissue)
hypoxia_driven_hTERT.effect: 2–4× hTERT expression boost during hypoxia, returning to basal at surface

osmotic_stress_response.mechanism: NFAT5/TonEBP binds engineered enhancers at hTERT locus
osmotic_stress_response.trigger: Hypertonic stress in marine gill epithelium (seawater: ~1000 mOsm)
osmotic_stress_response.effect: Compensatory hTERT upregulation to offset osmotically-induced oxidative attrition

cold_adaptation.mechanism: 3 surface Glu→Asp mutations reduce electrostatic rigidity at low temperature
cold_adaptation.effect: Processivity maintained at 4–15°C (vs. ~50% reduction in WT hTERT)
cold_adaptation.structural_basis: Increased conformational flexibility in the TRBD (telomerase RNA-binding domain)

salt_enhanced_G4.mechanism: Na⁺ (seawater) and K⁺ (intracellular) stabilize parallel G-quadruplex topology
salt_enhanced_G4.effect: G4 ceiling at ~12–13 kb is more robust in marine conditions
salt_enhanced_G4.note: Freshwater gilled humans have standard G4 stability

gilled_specific_enhancements.G_primitive_shift: 𐑲 → 𐑔 — tissue-specific (gill) rather than universal telomere maintenance

gill_epithelium.notes: High turnover in gill epithelium — primary target for ouroboric maintenance

other_somatic.notes: Standard somatic turnover; basal ouroboric maintenance
